Ad Text:
Group Executive Manager – Technology
A Strategic Technology Leadership Opportunity

Aptella is a recognised leader in intelligent positioning and automation solutions, partnering with customers to deliver innovation, precision, and measurable outcomes across complex industries.

We are seeking a commercially astute and strategically focused technology executive to join the Executive Leadership Team, reporting directly to the Chief Executive Officer. This role can be based in either Bundoora VIC or Silverwater NSW.

This is a unique opportunity to shape the future direction of technology, data, and digital capability within a growing and ambitious business.

The Role

The Group Executive Manager – Technology is accountable for defining and executing a cohesive technology strategy that enables growth, strengthens operational performance, and enhances customer value.

Operating at the intersection of CIO and CTO, the role spans enterprise technology, digital transformation, and innovation.

Key accountabilities include:

Setting and delivering a forward-looking Group Technology Strategy aligned to business objectives

Leading enterprise-wide digital transformation, including data, analytics, and AI initiatives

Ensuring technology platforms are secure, scalable, and aligned with business processes

Establishing robust governance across architecture, cybersecurity, and data management

Overseeing key enterprise systems, including Microsoft Dynamics 365

Partnering with business leaders to enable innovation in customer-facing solutions

As a trusted advisor to the CEO and Executive, you will play a central role in shaping strategy and driving organisational performance.

The Individual

You are an experienced technology executive with a track record of delivering enterprise-wide transformation and aligning technology with commercial outcomes.

You bring:

Experience in a senior technology leadership role (CIO, CTO or equivalent) within a complex environment

Demonstrated success in strategy development, transformation, and execution

Deep expertise across enterprise systems, cybersecurity, cloud, and data

Experience leading digital and AI-enabled initiatives that deliver tangible business value

Strong stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence at Executive and Board level

A balanced, pragmatic leadership style with a focus on outcomes, people, and performance

The Opportunity

This is a rare opportunity to join a high-performing leadership team and take ownership of a critical function during a period of growth and transformation.

You will be empowered to shape and lead the technology vision of the organisation, drive meaningful innovation and digital advancement, build capability across the business, and deliver measurable impact for both customers and the organisation.

To Apply

For a confidential discussion or to express interest, please submit your application via SEEK.

🔐 Business Analysts (Systems & Process) | TSPV Cleared
📍 Canberra (Onsite) | 💼 Permanent
💰 $150,000 - $190,000 (incl. super)

We are seeking experienced Business Analysis professionals to support a high-profile, classified program delivering critical national capability.

This is a rare opportunity to work in a Top Secret environment, contributing to complex ICT solutions that integrate and enhance existing systems using a reuse - buy - build approach. You'll play a key role in shaping outcomes where security, integration, and operational delivery intersect.

The Opportunity

You will join a collaborative program team working across business, technical, and security stakeholders to deliver end-to-end capability in a highly regulated environment.

We are hiring across:

Business Analysts (Process & Systems)
Senior Business Systems Analyst (SFIA Level 5 - Team Lead)
What You'll Be Doing

Core Responsibilities (All Roles):

Elicit and document business and system requirements through workshops, interviews, and analysis
Develop artefacts including user stories, process models, and interface designs
Analyse current systems and processes to inform future-state capability
Ensure traceability between requirements, existing capability, and delivered outcomes
Identify and manage risks, dependencies, and integration challenges

Senior Role (Team Lead):

Lead the Business Analysis function across a complex program
Guide reuse, buy, build decision-making for solution design
Mentor and coordinate a team of Business Analysts
Provide strategic analysis advice to Program leadership
Assure quality and consistency across all BA deliverables
What You'll Bring
Proven experience as a Business Analyst / Business Systems Analyst in complex ICT or digital programs
Strong stakeholder engagement across technical and non-technical audiences
Experience in secure, regulated, or government environments
Ability to analyse existing systems and processes to inform future solutions
Understanding of integrated environments (cloud/on-prem) and security considerations

For Senior (SFIA 5):

Demonstrated experience leading BA teams in complex delivery environments
Ability to shape solutions across reuse, COTS, and bespoke builds
Strong grasp of integration, data, infrastructure, or security concepts
Mandatory Requirements
Australian Citizenship
Current TSPV (Top Secret Positive Vetting) Clearance
Why Apply?
Work on mission-critical, classified programs
Influence complex solutions at a strategic level
Join a high-performing team delivering real national impact
Competitive salary with long-term career growth

If you're a TSPV-cleared Business Analyst looking to step into a high-impact role, apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion.

We’re seeking an AI Product & Transformation Lead to join a large, customer-focused organisation operating within a complex enterprise environment. This role suits a commercially minded operator who can bridge business and technology, take ownership of AI initiatives end-to-end, and ensure solutions are delivered, adopted, and delivering real impact.

In this role, you’ll be responsible for translating business problems into practical AI solutions, working closely with engineering and data teams to bring them to life, and driving adoption across the organisation. You will play a key role in ensuring AI moves beyond experimentation into embedded, value-generating capability.

Your day-to-day will include:

Identifying and prioritising high-value AI use cases across the business aligned to strategic objectives
Translating business needs into clear solution definitions and working with technical teams to deliver into production
Owning product roadmap, planning, and continuous optimisation of AI solutions
Redesigning workflows to enable effective human and AI collaboration
Driving adoption, change, and training to ensure solutions are used and deliver measurable outcomes
Engaging with stakeholders across multiple business units to align priorities and maximise value
Leading a small team focused on change, enablement, and training
About You:

You are an experienced transformation or product professional who combines strong commercial thinking with delivery capability. You are comfortable operating across business and technology, and have a track record of taking initiatives beyond concept into real-world outcomes.

Ideally, you’ll bring:

Proven experience delivering AI or automation solutions into production, not just strategy or proof of concepts
Background in consulting, digital transformation, or product leadership within complex organisations
Strong experience in stakeholder engagement, operating model change, and driving adoption
Experience owning product roadmaps, prioritisation, and value delivery
Solid understanding of modern AI concepts such as LLMs, automation, and workflow optimisation at a practical level
Ability to work closely with engineering and data teams without needing to be hands-on technical
A pragmatic, delivery-focused mindset with the ability to operate in ambiguity
If you’re seeking a role where you can take ownership of how AI is applied across a business, drive real transformation, and see tangible outcomes from your work, this position offers both impact and visibility.

If you are seeking your next position and would like to discuss this opportunity in further and expanded detail, please reach out directly to Dan Nyhuis at daniel.nyhuis@troocoo.com

Company: Cognizant Technology Solutions Australia Pty Ltd
Ad Text:
Cognizant (NASDAQ: CTSH) is a leading provider of information technology, consulting, and business process services, dedicated to helping the world's leading companies build stronger businesses. Headquartered in Teaneck, New Jersey (U.S.), Cognizant combines a passion for client satisfaction, technology innovation, deep industry and business process expertise, and a global, collaborative workforce that embodies the future of work. Cognizant is a member of the NASDAQ-100, the S&P 500, the Forbes Global 2000, and the Fortune 500 and is ranked among the top performing and fastest growing companies in the world.

Our Culture

Your passion, integrity and experience are integral to Cognizant's success. You will join a dynamic and expanding global leader in IT and Business consultancy where you will be valued for who you are. We take pride in our partnership with our clients, so your ability to add value and provide exceptional service to our clients is fundamental to your success. In return, you will be presented with opportunities to develop your career and collaborate with talented colleagues in a supportive, diverse environment.

At Cognizant we recognize that companies that are open and welcoming to a multi-culturally diverse workforce will thrive with fresh perspectives and collaborative knowledge. Cognizant focuses on promoting & increasing gender diversity and providing a workplace which encourages great participation and an equal playing field, where merit and accomplishment are the only criteria for success.

Job Summary:

We are seeking a Delivery Lead to lead end to end technical delivery for the GCP data platform ingestion transformations Big Query models ensuring outcomes meet agreed scope quality and timelines.

Responsibilities:

Act as the onshore customer facing delivery lead managing day to day coordination technical discussions dependency management and proactive risk issue handling.

Own solution design and technical governance across data ingestion transformation and Big Query data models ensuring scalability security and cost efficiency.

Experienced in translating business requirements into Technical functional specifications and co ordinate the technical deliverables with onshore and offshore team.

Facilitate Agile ceremonies manage sprint execution remove impediments and ensure backlog readiness and predictable delivery cadence.

Drive solution design and technical governance guide and mentor offshore onshore engineers enforcing engineering standards best practices and design consistency across the delivery.

Work closely with DevOps and testing teams to ensure production readiness CI CD compliance data quality gates and smooth releases.

Track and manage technical risks issues and dependencies proactively driving resolution to protect delivery commitments.

Provide clear technical status reporting and delivery inputs to program and service leadership supporting predictable and transparent outcomes.




Benefits:

Joining Cognizant will give you the opportunity to learn and collaborate with some of the most talented people in the industry, while having your finger on the pulse of emerging industry trends and working on the cutting edge of technology in your field of expertise.

We recognize that our people perform at their best when they feel valued as significant contributors and that is why at Cognizant, taking care of our employees is a priority:

·   You can pursue innovative career tracks and opportunities here

·   You can enhance your professional development through education and dedicated training

·   We’ll give you the skills you need to keep pace with the changing workplace while our compensation, benefits and wellness packages help you stay healthy and plan for the future.

Next Steps:

Please reach out to our friendly and welcoming team today to apply and register your interest for this full-time role. We'd love to help you get your next role and enable you to fulfil your professional ambitions.

Job Title: Senior Project Officer - Research Analysis, Data and Reporting
Ad Text:
The Opportunity

The Senior Project Officer will play a key role in supporting the delivery of UNSW’s strategy contributing to the development of simple, effective and trust-based systems, aligning and streamlining internal processes to allow researchers to focus on high-impact work.

This project role is hands-on rather than technical delivery. You’ll be responsible for planning, communications and documentation across a portfolio of concurrent initiatives (typically 10–20), ensuring stakeholders remain informed, aligned and on track. The role will proactively identify risks, overlaps and opportunities for greater efficiency across projects within a large, complex organisational environment.

You will work closely with a broad range of stakeholders to support project governance, maintain clear and consistent communications, and bring structure to complex and evolving initiatives. This includes preparing high-quality reports, briefings and presentations, and ensuring project documentation is accurate and up to date.

The role requires a confident, proactive approach, with the ability to ask questions and challenge where appropriate and bring clarity to ambiguity. You will be naturally curious and analytical, able to critically assess information and connect insights across multiple streams of work. 

About UNSW:

UNSW isn’t like other places you’ve worked. We are a large organisation with a diverse and highly talented community doing extraordinary work. We are driven to be thoughtful, practical and purposeful in all we do, and this collective approach is what gives our work impact. It is also why UNSW is consistently ranked among the world’s top 20 universities in the QS World University Rankings and is a proud member of Australia’s prestigious Group of Eight. If you’re seeking a career where you can thrive, be challenged and contribute to meaningful work, you’re in the right place.

Benefits and Culture: 

UNSW offer a competitive salary and access to benefits including:

17% superannuation and annual leave loading
Hybrid/Flexible working
Discounts and entitlements  
Affordable on-campus parking 

Who you are

You can demonstrate your experience in project management including the project life cycle and development of project plans, objectives and documentation.

You have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a high level of attention to detail for deliverables produced, and success working effectively and collaboratively on initiatives with a range of people at different levels within an organisation but also to work independently.

Strong capability in Excel, SharePoint, and PowerPoint is essential, as well as time management skills to respond to changing priorities, manage multiple tasks and meet competing deadlines by using judgement and initiative.

You’ll also be able to demonstrate your commitment to UNSW’s aims, objectives and values in action, together with relevant policies and guidelines, as well as health & safety (psychosocial and physical) responsibilities.

 To Apply: Make each day matter with a meaningful career at UNSW. Submit your application online before 11:55pm, Tuesday 28th May 2026. A copy of the Position Description can be found on JOB@UNSW.

Pre-Employment Checks 
Aligned with UNSW’s focus on cultivating a workplace defined by safety, ethical conduct, and strong integrity preferred candidates will be required to participate in a combination of pre-employment checks relevant to the role they have applied for.  

These pre-employment checks may include a combination of some of the following checks:-   

National and International Criminal history checks 
Entitlement to work and ID checks 
Working With Children Checks  
Completion of a Gender-Based Violence Prevention Declaration  
Verification of relevant qualifications 
Verification of relevant professional membership  
Employment history and reference checks 
Financial responsibility assessments/checks. 
Medical Checks and Assessments 

Compliance with the necessary combination of these checks is a condition of employment at UNSW. 

Contact: Shiree Thomas, Talent Acquisition Partner e: shiree.thomas@unsw.edu.au

Please apply through the application portal and not via the contact above. 

UNSW is committed to equity diversity and inclusion. Applications from women, people of culturally and linguistically diverse backgrounds, those living with disabilities, members of the LGBTIQ+ community; and people of Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander descent, are encouraged. UNSW provides workplace adjustments for people with disability, and access to flexible work options for eligible staff.

The University reserves the right not to proceed with any appointment.

Job Title: Senior Business Analyst (Quantatative Trading Compliance Manager).
Location: Pakenham, West Gippsland & Latrobe Valley VIC
Ad Text:
This role will see a highly quantitative individual with demonstrated programming experience and accounting qualifications to work collaboratively in a team of high performing, extremely analytical developers and other investment experts.

The successful candidate will be a key compliance manager working closely with traders to develop new algorithmic trading models, uplifting existing ones, as well as automating manual processes. This must also include integration with the company accounting ledgers to ensure compliance with Australian legislation. Keeping up to date with current accounting standards is also required. 
  
The best candidate will be analytical, curious and self-motivated with a keen eye for detail, and an analytical mind. They are an excellent communicator, in English and Hindi to in order to engage with offshore stakeholders and executive management. 

Sound Finance/ Banking/Investments experience

Agile experience

Excellent stakeholder and communications skills

Network Security.

Root cause analysis.

Strong attention to detail.

North Shore Sydney | Onsite Parking | Hybrid Working
Large, PE-backed Consumer Goods Business
10-Month Maximum Term Contract (Maternity Leave Cover)

Deliver high-quality, insightful reporting and “board-ready” presentations
Reporting & BAU

Ensure accuracy, timeliness, and clarity of financial outputs
Partner closely with Finance Business Partners and Controllership teams
Projects & Continuous Improvement

Build and enhance financial models and planning tools
Support strategic initiatives through financial analysis and modelling
Drive process improvements across reporting, planning and systems
  
About You

Proven FP&A experience (Manager level or strong Senior Analyst with leadership potential ready to step up)
CA/CPA advantageous (but not a deal-breaker)
Strong financial modelling, forecasting and analytical capability
Experience in corporate/group reporting within a multi-site or complex environment
Excellent stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, evolving environment
Experience in consumer products or manufacturing is advantageous.
  
What’s on Offer

High-profile role with direct CFO exposure
Lead a capable and supportive team
Work for a well-established, industry-leading organisation
Hybrid working (approx. 3 days onsite)
On-site parking
Opportunity to make a meaningful impact during a key business period

Apply Now
To apply, please click on the appropriate link below. For a confidential discussion, please contact Samantha Tan at stan@sharpandcarter.com.au

The Role
We are seeking a commercially astute and strategic Head of Financial Planning & Analysis (FP&A) to lead a high-performing function within a large, complex organisation.  This is a pivotal leadership role responsible for driving enterprise-wide financial performance, delivering best-in-class planning, forecasting, and insights, and leading a significant transformation agenda.
  
You will play a critical role in shaping business strategy, embedding a “one business” mindset, and enabling data-driven decision-making across the organisation.
  
Leading a team spanning FP&A, business insights, and performance analytics, you will partner closely with senior executives to optimise performance, identify opportunities, and drive sustainable growth.
  

Lead enterprise-wide business performance and transformation initiatives
Drive a culture of continuous improvement, efficiency, and innovation
Oversee complex, multi-stream programs ensuring alignment, delivery, and measurable outcomes
Identify and implement initiatives to enhance operational and financial performance

To apply online, please click on the appropriate link below. Alternatively, for a confidential discussion, please contact Jonathon Prince.

Optus is an Australian telecommunications company, delivering more than 11 million services to our customers every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.

We're looking for a Senior AI Product Manager to lead major product initiatives across our Intelligent Contacts and AI agent portfolio. This is a highly impactful role where you'll shape the future of AI‑powered customer experiences, driving strategy, delivery and real business outcomes at scale.

What You'll Do

Own the product vision and strategy for a portfolio of AI products and agents
Lead product discovery, customer research, experimentation, and market analysis
Define and track AI success metrics, balancing model performance KPIs with business outcomes
Translate business problems into ML solutions, assessing feasibility and trade‑offs across accuracy, latency, and cost
Influence model architecture and agent design, including prompts, RAG, fine‑tuning, and escalation logic
Monitor and optimise model performance using production data, including drift and hallucination mitigation
Use contact centre analytics (CSAT, AHT, FCR, transfers) to measure impact and identify opportunities
Partner with Engineering and Data Science, lead cross‑functional initiatives, own ROI, and champion responsible AI

What makes you perfect

7+ years' experience in Product Management, including 3-5 years delivering AI/ML‑powered products at scale
Proven track record delivering large, high‑impact product initiatives
Strong technical foundation in AI/ML, including generative AI, LLMs, NLP, and transformers
Hands‑on experience with model evaluation metrics, prompt engineering, RAG, embeddings, fine‑tuning, and MLOps
Familiarity with leading ML and LLM platforms (e.g. Vertex AI, Azure OpenAI, Bedrock)
Strong data and analytical capability, including SQL, Python, experimentation, and performance analysis
Sound understanding of responsible AI, including governance, ethics, safety, and regulatory compliance
Confidence influencing senior stakeholders, with experience in complex or contact‑centre/customer operations environments

What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.
Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.
Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym and on-site childcare centre.
Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.
We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.
Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.
Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).
Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

At Optus, we are strengthened by others and that means valuing diversity and saying ‘yes' to embracing individual differences. We are committed to ensuring that our application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, individuals with a disability, individuals identifying as being part of the LGBTQIA+ community, individuals who may have served in the armed forces or who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive. Are you a data leader who thrives on turning complexity into clarity? Interfi Systems is looking for a Lead Data Engineer to own our data strategy, drive client onboarding success, and build a world-class data team from the ground up.

This is a rare opportunity to step into a pivotal role at a growing fintech, where your technical vision and leadership will directly shape how we deliver for clients and scale our platform.

About Interfi Systems

Interfi Systems is a Sydney-based fintech delivering the Capital platform — a core banking and financial services solution trusted by clients across the industry. We're scaling fast and looking for leaders who want to make a real impact.

The Role

As Lead Data Engineer, you'll report directly to the COO and take ownership of data transformation, governance, and client migration across the business. You'll lead a team of data engineers, act as the technical escalation point, and translate complex business requirements into a clear data roadmap.

What You'll Be Doing

• Leading, growing, and mentoring the Data and Conversions team — including hiring, onboarding, and performance management

• You will be expected to manage a team of 3-5 data engineers, providing hands-on leadership and strategic direction.

• Directing data migration and conversion activities for client onboarding projects onto the Capital platform

• Designing and implementing data warehouse / data lake architecture for high performance and scalability

• Building and maintaining robust ETL/ELT pipelines for structured and unstructured data

• Establishing and enforcing data governance policies, standards, and data quality frameworks

• Ensuring compliance with data privacy regulations (GDPR, ACCC, APRA) and managing RBAC

• Designing reporting solutions that drive business insights and operational decision-making

• Collaborating cross-functionally with engineering, service management, and client-facing teams

What We're Looking For

Essential:

• 5+ years in data management, data engineering, or database administration, with 2+ years in a team lead role

• Strong SQL proficiency including complex queries, stored procedures, and performance tuning

• Hands-on experience with SQL Server is essential for this role.

• Demonstrated experience managing data migration and conversion projects

• Solid understanding of data governance frameworks and data quality principles

• Experience designing and working with ETL/ELT pipelines

• Strong communication skills — able to translate data concepts for non-technical stakeholders

Preferred:

• Background in financial services, fintech, or core banking data environments

• Familiarity with C#, Python or scripting languages for data automation

• Exposure to cloud data platforms (Azure preferred)

• Experience with Power BI, SSRS or similar reporting tools

• Knowledge of data privacy regulations and compliance requirements

Why Join Us?

• Be a founding leader of a growing data practice

• Work directly with the COO on strategy and roadmap

• Sydney CBD office with a collaborative, high-performance culture

If you're ready to own data at scale and lead a team that delivers, we'd love to hear from you.

Solve by Talent is partnering with the University of Sydney to recruit a Senior ICT Business Partner (Research) to support the delivery of key Information and Communications Technology program (ICT) initiatives that help shape the future of education, research, and innovation.  
  
The Research function enables world-class discovery by partnering with research teams to deliver the digital tools, platforms, and services that support the full research lifecycle. From improving access to data and systems to enhancing collaboration, compliance, and research outcomes, this work helps strengthen the University’s global research impact. 
  
Why join the University of Sydney? 
  
Join one of Australia’s leading universities at a pivotal point of change, where technology is evolving to better support student learning, empower teaching excellence, and enable research delivery at global scale. 
  
ICT is central to delivering the University’s academic mission. Working across faculties, disciplines, and research communities, you will shape the platforms, services, and partnerships that enable student success, strengthen teaching, and accelerate research impact. 
  
This work spans both the critical services that keep the University running every day and the ongoing improvement of systems and experiences. 
  
With strong investment and clear direction, you’ll operate in a large and complex environment where your contribution has visible, meaningful impact across the University. 
  
You’ll collaborate across academic and professional communities to help shape a more connected and capable University, supporting teaching excellence, research impact, and student success. 
  
The opportunity 
  
As Senior ICT Business Partner, you will act as a key strategic interface between ICT and the broader University, building trusted relationships with senior stakeholders and helping align technology services, solutions and initiatives with business priorities.  
  
You will bring together stakeholder engagement, planning, delivery oversight and service improvement, helping shape demand, translate needs into actionable requirements, and ensure ICT delivers value in a way that is practical, collaborative and outcome focused.  
  
What you’ll do 

Partner with senior stakeholders to understand priorities, challenges and future needs, and align ICT responses accordingly  
Translate business needs into high-level ICT requirements, roadmap inputs, business cases and delivery priorities

Demonstrated ability to partner with senior academic and professional stakeholders to shape priorities, influence decisions, and drive meaningful outcomes  
Experience working in complex institutional environments, navigating governance, competing priorities, and cross-functional stakeholder groups

What’s in it for you? 
  
At the University of Sydney, we support our staff with a range of benefits designed to enhance your career, wellbeing and work-life balance, including: 

Up to 17% superannuation to support your financial future 
Extremely generous parental leave entitlements. 
Competitive salary with salary packaging options 
Flexible and hybrid working arrangements 
Career development and learning opportunities 
Health and wellbeing programs including Fitness Passport 
Access to a vibrant, globally recognised university community 
How to apply

Accessibility and inclusion 
  
The University of Sydney is committed to creating an inclusive recruitment experience for all candidates. If you require adjustments or additional support at any stage of the recruitment process, we encourage you to let us know. This may include adjustments to the interview format, additional time, breaks, accessible interview locations, receiving materials in advance, or any other practical support that will help you participate comfortably. 
  
Pre-employment checks 
  
Your employment is conditional upon the completion of all role required pre-employment or background checks in terms satisfactory to the University. Similarly, your ongoing employment is conditional upon the satisfactory maintenance of all relevant clearances and background check requirements. If you do not meet these conditions, the University may take any necessary step, including the termination of your employment. 
  
Also, to meet the University’s obligations under the National Higher Education Code to Prevent and Eliminate Gender-Based Violence you will be asked to declare if you have been investigated for, or found to engaged in, sexual harm or gender-based violence in the course of previous employment or in a legal process. 
  
Sponsorship / work rights for Australia 
  
You must have unrestricted work rights in Australia for the duration of this employment to be eligible to apply. Visa sponsorship is not available for this appointment.

About us

Boeing Defence Australia is shaping the future of aerospace and delivering some of the nation's most important programs for the Australian Defence Force.

Joining us is a chance to make your mark, working with a diverse team that is united in pushing the boundaries of imagination and excellence.

We currently employ more than 2,500 people across 14 sites in Australia and three international locations and have a range of opportunities available as we plan for future growth.

The opportunity

We are seeking a capable engineer or analyst to join the Data Analytics team in Digital Engineering. Our team plays a critical role in delivering data driven insights and scalable data solutions to support key Defence objectives. With an initial focus on establishing reliable data flows into the data warehouse, this role will also contribute across the full data analytics lifecycle, from stakeholder engagement through to delivery of impactful visualisations and AI ready data products.  The role offers technical mentoring within a supportive team, and opportunities to progress into advanced analytics, AI enablement and broader platform engineering activities. 

Responsibilities:

Collaborate with cross functional teams to understand data needs and deliver insights and fit for purpose data solutions.
Undertake data wrangling, extraction, transformation and loading utilising Python and SQL.
Develop and maintain reliable data pipelines and ETL/ELT processes in Apache Airflow that connect large datasets from a variety of structured and unstructured sources.
Utilise dbt for data modelling and transformation.
Support development and maintenance of data platform infrastructure, including data warehouses, data lakes and, where required, data access APIs.
Build visualisations in Tableau.
Develop data applications in Python/HTML/CSS.
Work with stakeholders to define requirements, support delivery, and promote data standards, reuse, scalability, stability and operational efficiency.
Contribute to automation and CI/CD practices to improve scalability, repeatability and reliability of data and analytics solutions.

Experience/Qualifications

Essential:

Bachelor's degree in a technical field (e.g., Software Engineering, Computer Science, Physics, Mathematics) or equivalent industry experience.
Demonstrated experience in Python.
Ability to work with SQL (knowledge will be valued; training will be provided).
Understanding of data modelling principles.
Experience or strong interest in building and maintaining data pipelines and transforming data from multiple sources.
Ability to work collaboratively with stakeholders to understand requirements and deliver high quality data products.
Strong problem solving skills and attention to data quality, reliability and documentation.

Desirable:

Defence and/or Aerospace domain knowledge.
Experience with dbt modelling and Apache Airflow.
Familiarity with MLOps and CI/CD practices.
Experience with Tableau.
Experience with Docker.
Experience with cloud platforms (e.g., AWS, Azure, GCP).
Experience with data warehousing, data lakes, or data platform infrastructure.
Familiarity with data quality monitoring, observability and pipeline testing practices.
Understanding of security, governance and access control considerations in enterprise data environments.
Analytical aptitude demonstrated through previous projects.

The Opportunity
Are you a highly experienced Architect who views SAP as a powerful engine? Do you champion "Clean Core" principles and thrive on solving the complexities of a large-scale retail and supply chain ecosystem?

The Role

End-to-End Design: Lead architecture across Finance, SCM, Retail, and HR, ensuring traceability from business drivers to data flow.

Integration & Security: Drive API-first and event-driven patterns to reduce point-to-point dependencies, aligning with Microsoft Entra ID and ServiceNow.

10+ years of Enterprise Solution Architecture experience.
Deep SAP Expertise: Proven experience with S/4HANA, BTP, MDG, and SuccessFactors, but with an "Enterprise-First" mindset.

This role is intended for seasoned Architects. It isn't suitable for functional consultants or configuration specialists.

Job Title: Product Manager - Global Share Plans & Tax
Ad Text:
About Us 

Automic Group is Australia’s market leader in technology-driven investor administration. Combining proprietary cloud-native technology with expert service, Automic simplifies investor administration and governance for companies and funds. It provides the market's leading registry, employee share plans, IR analytics, and fund administration services for over 1,400 ASX-listed and unlisted organisations. Founded to challenge an industry reliant on outdated systems, Automic is committed to delivering smarter, more efficient, and secure solutions that help Australia’s companies and fund managers. 

Automic’s culture is collaborative, inclusive, and purpose-driven. It is grounded in our mission to connect companies and funds to their investors through technology, service, and trust, and guided by our values of One Automic, Go Beyond, Empowered, and Accountable.




About the Role

As Product Manager for Global Share Plans & Tax, you will play a key strategic role in shaping Automic’s product direction in line with market trends, customer needs, and long-term business growth. You’ll act as the voice of the customer, bringing together market insights, stakeholder input, and cross-functional collaboration to develop and deliver differentiated product strategies. You will operate as a senior product thinker, balancing commercial impact, innovation, and product excellence. A core part of the role is ensuring secure-by-design practices and embedding ISMS compliance across the entire product lifecycle.

Key Responsibilities include: 

Shape and evolve the long-term product vision using market insights, customer needs, and strategic opportunity assessment.
Lead Voice of Customer programs and strategic client engagements to inform roadmap decisions and strengthen product-market fit.
Influence and partner cross-functionally with Sales, Delivery, Engineering, Marketing, and executives to drive aligned product direction.
Provide end-to-end product management for key product streams, including strategy, prioritisation, backlog ownership, and delivery outcomes.
Build and embed scalable product frameworks covering prioritisation, roadmap planning, continuous discovery, and outcome measurement.
Ensure secure-by-design practices and ISMS/ISO 27001 alignment across the product lifecycle through close collaboration with Cybersecurity and Risk.
Represent product vision to executives, clients, and external stakeholders, contributing to sales enablement and industry presence.
Drive innovation by identifying automation, AI-assisted workflows, and opportunities to reduce manual processes and improve efficiency.



About you

To be successful in this role, you will have:

5+ years in product management or product ownership roles, ideally within financial or regulated environments.
Strong understanding of AASB2/IFRS2 share-based payment reporting and adjacent compliance workflows.
You have hands-on experience with Global Employee Share Plans and associated tax reporting processes.
Experience delivering investor or client-facing platforms.
Strategic thinker with excellent stakeholder engagement skills.
Comfortable working in regulated reporting environments (ASIC, APRA, ATO).

Why join Automic

You’ll be part of a team where operational excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement are genuinely valued. This is an opportunity to deepen your expertise, contribute to meaningful outcomes, and grow your career within a high-performing, purpose-led fintech environment.

Key Benefits include:

Hybrid working
Professional career growth & development
Supportive & fast paced environment
Opportunity to grow with the business

If the above sounds like you, please apply for this exciting opportunity and be part of the growth journey with Automic!

As RACT continues to transform and create more value for Tasmanians, we have a unique opportunity for someone to join our Transformation team as our Manager Portfolio Insights.

RACT’s Change Agenda is growing in scale, complexity, and expectation. The Manager Portfolio Insights is a newly created role with a unique opportunity to build, from the ground up, the analytical intelligence, visualisation capability, and decision-support infrastructure that will underpin how the ELT and GCEO prioritise, resource, and govern change across the organisation.

Some of your accountabilities will include:

Design and maintain RACT’s first integrated portfolio view of all active and pipeline initiatives, including status, budget, resourcing, dependencies, and strategic alignment.

Develop an internal rate card for PMO resourcing and establish a portfolio benefits register in collaboration with Finance and project sponsors, tracking realisation against approved business cases.

Design high-quality portfolio visualisations that translate complex, multi-dimensional data into clear narratives for the GCEO, ELT, and Board, making prioritisation trade-offs immediately legible to senior decision-makers.

Develop and maintain interactive dashboards providing real-time visibility of portfolio health, financial performance, delivery risk, and capacity utilisation.

Prepare concise, decision-ready briefing materials for governance forums and ELT prioritisation discussions.

Build the analytical evidence base for portfolio prioritisation, modelling trade-offs across strategic alignment, delivery capacity, risk, cost, and benefit to support more rigorous ELT decision-making.

Contribute to continuous improvement of PMO frameworks, standards, and tools; coach stakeholders in interpreting portfolio data and maintaining planning accuracy.

A copy of the full position description can be provided by emailing peopleandculture@ract.com.au

Successful candidates will have:

5 or more years’ experience in portfolio management, PMO, or strategic analytics, with demonstrated ability to build reporting, insight, and decision‑support capabilities.

Advanced analytical and data visualisation capability, including capacity modelling, scenario planning, and benefits tracking, using Power BI, Tableau, or equivalent tools.

Proven experience producing clear, decision‑grade materials for executive leadership and board audiences.

Strong understanding of delivery environments, including experience working with technology functions and agile or hybrid lifecycles.

High proficiency in Excel, PowerPoint, Jira, and MS Project (or equivalent enterprise tools).

Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information into concise, compelling portfolio narratives.

Relevant qualifications or certification in business, data analytics, or project management, with experience in regulated, financial, or member‑based organisations viewed favourably.

This role is a 12 Month FTC with a view to go permanent. Reporting to the Head of FP&A, the FP&A Reporting Manager will contribute to core group planning and reporting activities, while directing the majority of their focus toward transformation. In the near term that means improving the tools, models, and processes that underpin FP&A — driver-based forecasting, reporting automation, planning system enhancements, and better data visibility across the group.

Within six months, the role is expected to transition into a full-time FP&A representative on an ERP implementation — working directly with finance, IT, and the implementation partner to ensure the new system is built around how the business actually plans, forecasts, and reports.

If you're someone who finds the build more interesting than the BAU, this role is worth a conversation.

What You'll Bring

Solid group or divisional FP&A experience, ideally in consumer, FMCG, or retail
Demonstrated experience implementing FP&A tools and processes — forecasting models, reporting automation, planning system enhancements
Hands-on experience with planning and BI platforms such as TM1, Anaplan, Power BI, or equivalent is ideal
Exposure to ERP environments and how finance data flows through integrated systems — prior ERP implementation experience preferred but not essential.
A genuine process improvement mindset with the confidence to challenge how things are currently done
Clear communicator who can work effectively across finance and with IT and project stakeholders
What's on Offer

A role with genuine scope beyond the day-to-day FP&A cycle
Clear pathway onto a high-visibility ERP project within six months
A business actively investing in its finance function
Competitive remuneration package commensurate with experience

Benefits

Fantastic ongoing grade 11/12 technical leadership role driving the insider risk stream, eDiscovery capabilities, and operational excellence.

Accomplished cyber security professional with over 7 years of experience, specialising in insider risk management, threat detection, and behavioural analytics.
Proven track record in leading large-scale digital investigations, designing insider risk detection workflows, and executing complex eDiscovery strategies.
Expert in eDiscovery, insider risk platforms, UEBA, digital forensics and data analysis across diverse operating systems and file types.

Partner with internal and external stakeholders to resolve technical challenges, deploy advanced security capabilities, and strengthen organisational resilience.

About the Role

We're looking for a highly skilled and experienced Senior SAP Functional Analyst to join our dynamic IT team at our Daikin Park in Warwick Farm, NSW. In this role, you will analyse the business problems and requirements to design a fully functional solution, assisting the Enterprise Architect to evaluate and finalise the SAP solutions. This is a fantastic opportunity to make a significant impact within a leading global company.

Your key responsibilities will include:


SAP System Expertise & Support: Leading the entire lifecycle of SAP Supply Chain Management (SCM) module (including WM, MM, PP) implementation and optimisation. You'll translate business needs into effective SAP solutions to enhance the supply chain, improve inventory, and optimise production, ensuring seamless integration with other SAP systems and providing functional expertise throughout project phases.

Business Process Analysis and Optimisation: Focuses on optimising supply chain management processes by analysing current operations and recommending improvements. You'll collaborate closely with business users to gather and document their requirements, then map these needs to existing or new SAP functionalities. A key objective is to identify and implement process automation and efficiency gains to streamline operations and reduce costs.


SAP Configuration and Customisation: Involved in the configuration of SAP modules like SCM (Core WM, MM and PP) to align with business requirements and best practices. You'll also develop functional specifications for custom developments, collaborating closely with the technical team. A key responsibility is to conduct and support testing phases (unit, integration, and UAT) to ensure SAP solutions function correctly and perform optimally.

Problem Resolution & Optimisation: Act as a key resource for troubleshooting and resolving critical SAP incidents and problems. You'll perform root cause analysis, implement corrective actions, and proactively identify areas for system optimisation to enhance performance and user experience.

Project Leadership & Collaboration: Play a vital role in SAP-related projects, including upgrades, migrations, and new module implementations. You'll work seamlessly with other IT teams (e.g., Basis, ABAP developers), external consultants, and business users to deliver successful outcomes.




We're looking for someone who brings:

You're a highly experienced and technically proficient SAP professional with a deep understanding of supply chain processes and a passion for optimizing systems. You're a natural problem-solver, an excellent communicator, and thrive in a dynamic, multicultural environment.

We're looking for someone who brings:

Higher education in Computer Sciences / Any Engineering Studies, and related SAP certification is mandatory.

Minimum of 10 years of hands-on/proven experience in SAP SCM functional roles covering Materials Management (MM), Production Planning (PP), Sales and Distribution (SD), and Warehouse Management (WM).

Proven experience in full life-cycle SAP implementations, including gathering requirements, configuring systems, conducting testing, and supporting go-live.

Experience in S/4HANA System conversion is a must.

Proficient and strong understanding of supply chain processes such as procurement, inventory management, production planning, and order fulfilment.

Knowledge of SQL and PL/SQL, with experience in Manhattan Scale highly preferred.


Ability to write functional specifications for custom developments and support ABAP teams as needed.

Experience with SAP interfaces, integrations, and EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) with Manhattan Scale is beneficial.




Why Join Us?

Collaborative work environment fostering a positive team culture.

Competitive salary + Superannuation + Performance bonus.

Equipment for work will be provided, such as a work laptop and work phone.

Provided with on-the-job training for continuous career growth opportunities.

Discounted Bupa Health Fund.

Flare Benefits – Discounted gift cards, novated leases, discounted health/car/home loans.

Be part of a global number 1 company in HVAC.

Be part of a dynamic team that values innovation, collaboration, and personal growth.

Free parking on site at our Warwick Farm, Daikin Park.




We thank all applicants for their interest; however, only those selected for an interview will be contacted.

Daikin Australia is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.

Job Title: Lecturer/Senior Lecturer, Psychology and Cognitive Neuroscience
Ad Text:
About the Role 
Swinburne is seeking a Lecturer or Senior Lecturer in Psychology and Cognitive Neuroscience to join our high-performing academic team. This is a unique opportunity to contribute to world-class teaching, impactful research, and innovative curriculum design within a collaborative and future-focused environment.

Depending on your experience, you will contribute at either Lecturer or Senior Lecturer level across teaching, research, and curriculum development in cognitive neuroscience. In this role, you will:

Deliver engaging undergraduate and postgraduate teaching
Design and enhance contemporary, industry-relevant curriculum
Conduct high-quality, impactful research
Supervise undergraduate, postgraduate, and higher degree research students
Contribute to academic administration and professional activities

At Senior Lecturer level, you will also:

Lead courses and coordinate teaching teams
Provide academic leadership and mentorship to colleagues and students
Foster stronger links with industry and external partners (national and international)
Create and expand opportunities for student engagement in research beyond coursework

About the Department
The Department of Psychological Sciences, within the School of Health Sciences, is recognised for excellence in both teaching and research. Our research is nationally and internationally recognised and we continue to advance innovation in psychology and cognitive neuroscience.

We work closely with the Centre for Mental Health and Brain Sciences and Swinburne’s world-leading Neuroimaging Facility—the only single-site human imaging facility in Australia and New Zealand offering MEG, MRI, and EEG.


About You  
You are a collaborative and motivated academic with expertise in cognitive neuroscience and a passion for teaching and research. For both levels of appointment, you will bring:

Proven research experience using cognitive neuroscience methodologies (e.g. MRI, MEG, EEG)
A strong (Lecturer) or established (Senior Lecturer) track record of publications
Experience teaching in a university setting
Ability to contribute across a broad range of undergraduate and postgraduate offerings
Excellent communication skills and a collaborative approach

In addition, at Senior Lecturer level you will demonstrate:

Leadership experience in higher education, including course or program coordination
Experience supervising undergraduate research projects and fostering student research engagement
A strong ability to build and maintain industry and disciplinary links
A track record of mentoring staff and contributing to a positive team culture

Qualifications
A PhD or equivalent doctoral degree in psychology or related discipline.


About Swinburne University of Technology 
Swinburne’s strategy draws upon our understanding of future challenges. We choose to build Swinburne as the prototype of a new and different university – one that is truly of Technology, of Innovation, and of Entrepreneurship. We are committed to a differentiated university proposition in education and research.

Our Ad Astra strategy is the cornerstone of Swinburne’s bold ambition to lead globally in technology-driven education and research. This strategy positions us to create transformative solutions, empower learners, and partner with industry to thrive in an ever-changing world.   


What We Offer You!

Work your way with flexible working arrangements, including hybrid options and generous parental leave to support a balanced lifestyle.
Exclusive staff discounts, including discounted Medibank health insurance, 50% off Swinburne courses, and other partner offers.
Swinburne offers 17% super and salary packaging options, including car leasing and extra super contributions.

Want to learn more? Head over to our benefits page!


To Apply 
Please submit your CV and cover letter addressing the Key Selection Criteria and your suitability for this position.  

To review the Position Description and Key Selection Criteria and to apply, please scroll down to the bottom of the page. 

If you are viewing this advert from an external site, please click ‘apply’ and you will be redirected to Swinburne’s Jobs website to access the Position Description and Key Selection Criteria at the bottom of the page.

Please Note: Appointment to this position is subject to passing a Working with Children Check.

If you are experiencing technical difficulties with your application, please contact the Talent Acquisition team at talentacquisition@swin.edu.au 


Applications Close: Sunday 17th of May, at 11.00pm

Swinburne offers flexible working options contained in our leave and parenting/carer policies to support work-life balance.


Diversity, Equity and Inclusion
Swinburne has become a world-class university, driving social and economic impacts through science, technology, and innovation. As a dual-sector university, our vision is for people and technology working together to build a better world.

Central to our vision is our commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion. We pride ourselves on being an equal opportunity employer focused on attracting, retaining, and developing great talent. We work to remove barriers related to gender identity, culture, ethnicity, sexual orientation, disability, and age.

We strongly encourage applicants from diverse Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities. Our Moondani Toombadool Centre leads our Indigenous education and culture at Swinburne, guided by community wisdom and leadership.

We support applicants with disabilities. Adjustments can be requested at any time during the recruitment process. For Reasonable Adjustment requests, including accessible formats for the PD, application form or any other document, please contact DCR@swin.edu.au or call +61 3 9214 3550.

Please note the above number and DCR email address are for disability or reasonable adjustments queries only. General enquiries about the role can be sent to the Talent Acquisition team at talentacquisition@swin.edu.au (general enquiries will not be answered by phone).

Ad Text:
Permanent Opportunity
Hybrid Work Environment
Lead a delivery practice supporting enterprise integration solutions for national critical systems


About the Role


As a Practice Manager supporting the Integration Services team at the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA), you will lead, coach and develop team members while working closely with project managers, technical professionals, architecture and security teams to achieve agreed delivery outcomes.


You will be responsible for establishing and maintaining standard work practices and integration design standards, including automation, to improve the quality, maturity and consistency of services, technologies and supporting processes.


Key Responsibilities


Develop, lead and mature the Integration Services delivery practice by providing people leadership, building capability, setting direction and priorities, managing demand forecasts with supply and contributing to resourcing strategies that enable high quality delivery outcomes.
Ensure operational accountability for the delivery practice by overseeing performance, compliance and adherence to agreed processes and controls.
Provide technical delivery guidance and assurance across integration initiatives, ensuring solutions meet agreed standards for quality, consistency, reliability, and operational readiness.
Partner closely with project managers, architecture, security, operations, and vendors to guide solution design, align expectations, support accurate estimation and forecasting, resolve delivery and technical risks, and achieve sustainable outcomes.
Influence and promote the adoption of common integration patterns, platforms, and automation approaches to improve reuse, efficiency, and long term maintainability.
Contribute to the development, implementation, and continuous improvement of integration standards, policies, systems, and delivery practices, ensuring alignment with enterprise architecture, industry standards, and organisational requirements.
Ensure integration solutions are delivered and transitioned to meet operational needs, supportability, sustainability, enabling effective transition into business as usual operations.
Manage stakeholder and vendor relationships, ensuring procurement and delivery engagements are effective, expectations are aligned, and issues or blockers impacting delivery outcomes are addressed in a timely manner.
Proactively identify, assess, and respond to delivery, technical, and operational risks and opportunities, maintaining delivery momentum while protecting service quality and stability.
Provide clear assurance, insight, and reporting on integration delivery outcomes, risks, and improvement opportunities to senior stakeholders.


About You


You bring strong people and practice leadership experience, including developing capability, setting clear expectations, and fostering a culture of collaboration, innovation, and accountability.
You are an experienced technology and delivery leader with a proven ability to influence technical outcomes across complex initiatives, balancing delivery priorities with long term sustainability, operability, and risk management.
You have a solid understanding of integration technologies and patterns across hybrid environments, including APIs, data and application integration, and middleware platforms, and are comfortable guiding and challenging solution design without being hands‑on.
You bring experience implementing, evolving, or scaling enterprise integration platforms in complex environments, including technologies such as IBM Sterling Integrator, MQ, MuleSoft, Azure Logic Apps, and Azure API Management.
You are experienced in governing and evolving delivery practices, standards, patterns, and automation approaches to improve quality, consistency, and efficiency across multiple initiatives.
You work effectively with technical professionals within your practice, as well as project managers, architecture, operations, and vendors, influencing decisions and resolving delivery and technical risks.
You bring a strong understanding of Financial Services, Government, or similarly regulated environments, including relevant policies, risk frameworks, and compliance obligations, and can guide teams to deliver compliant outcomes.
You understand the importance of operational readiness, and can ensure solutions are supportable, reliable, and fit for ongoing operation.
You bring a pragmatic understanding of portfolio and project delivery, applying waterfall and agile approaches to enable effective delivery rather than enforcing methodology.
You have strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills, with experience providing assurance and clear advice to senior leaders.


Be More

Working for an organisation that truly makes a difference to the people of Australia, we can offer development and career opportunities in a collaborative environment that supports your growth, wellbeing and promotes flexibility. Your individual growth and success drives the RBA forward as an organisation. Be more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.


Why RBA?

The RBA makes an important contribution to the Australian economy through the pursuit of national economic policy objectives and associated activities in financial markets and banking. We also issue Australia's banknotes and operate infrastructure critical to the payments system, all of which contribute to the welfare of the Australian people. Made up of specialists across a wide range of fields, our people, values, and culture play a critical role in achieving our objectives. Striving to be Open & Dynamic, we consider and incorporate different perspectives, work across teams and are transparent with each other, whilst delivering quality together effectively and focusing on outcomes by prioritising, testing, learning, and refining as we go. Our people conduct themselves with a high degree of integrity, while striving for excellence in the work they perform and the outcomes they achieve. We encourage intelligent inquiry and we treat one another with respect while promoting the public interest through our efforts. We know it is the growth and success of our people that drives the RBA forward. Come and make a bigger contribution while you build and develop your own skills too, because being more means you can do more, for yourself and for Australia.

The Reserve Bank of Australia is committed to equity, diversity and inclusion through key initiatives. We welcome and encourage applicants from diverse backgrounds to apply, including Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, culturally and linguistically diverse background, those living with a disability and from the LGBTQ+ community. We are committed to making the recruitment process fair and equitable for all our candidates.

Ad Text:
The Opportunity

Join a high‑performing Insurance Capability Team that plays a critical role in strengthening the performance, sustainability and integrity of the ReturntoWork Scheme in South Australia.

As the Insurance Capability, Strategic Advisor, you will lead strategic, scheme‑wide capability initiatives that enable Insurance leaders and teams - including our Claims Agents - to deliver improved outcomes for injured workers and employers. This role is central to identifying capability risks, designing practical and strategic solutions, and embedding systems that build a skilled, sustainable workforce across the Scheme.

Working closely with ReturntoWorkSA leaders, Claims Agents, internal business units and external stakeholders, you will turn strategic intent into operational reality. You will design and operationalise capability frameworks, lead complex projects, and provide strategic insights that support informed decision‑making across the Insurance function.

If you are energised by strategic problem‑solving, enjoy influencing at senior levels and want to make a meaningful impact on the performance of the Scheme, we encourage you to apply.

This role is offered as a two-year, full-time contract position.

The Position

As an Insurance Capability, Strategic Advisor, you will:

Optimise Insurance Capability

Design and maintain strategic systems that identify and analyse capability gaps across Insurance and the broader Scheme
Lead strategic initiatives that address capability risks and strengthen operational performance
Contribute to the design and review of key claims management systems and tools
Develop practical resources to support the application of legislative tests
Research best‑practice capability approaches across comparable schemes
Partner with People & Culture to identify talent needs and workforce risks

Enable Insurance Leadership Strategy

Work with Insurance leaders to evaluate scheme performance and identify emerging risks
Provide insights, tools, forums and data that support timely and informed strategic decisions
Support leaders to build strategic capability and deliver on Insurance strategic

Capability Data Management & Analysis

Establish and maintain robust capability and customer service data systems including Insurance Customer Experience
Produce monthly reporting and work with leaders to analyse trends and emerging risks
Develop and enable strategies to address identified risks

About You

You are a proactive and experienced strategic leader who is motivated by improving performance through people, systems and capability. You bring a balance of analytical thinking, operational understanding and strong stakeholder influence.

You will bring:

About ReturnToWorkSA

At ReturnToWorkSA, our purpose is to empower and support South Australians impacted by work injury. Our services and those delivered by our claims agents are designed to provide early intervention support to workers and employers following a work injury to ensure the worker can recover and return to work as quickly as possible. We manage the collection of premium payments from registered employers and our invested funds to ensure we maintain a viable financial position that enables the delivery of quality support and services to injured workers.

In addition, we are the regulator of the Return to Work Scheme and play the key role of protecting the integrity and fairness of the Scheme by monitoring and enforcing compliance with the Act and providing education and support about the health benefits of work.

Why join us? 

Experience a workplace culture that rewards your contribution and provides the development and support to help you reach your potential. We are committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace where our people can genuinely make a difference to the lives of South Australians affected by work injury. At ReturnToWorkSA, you can expect a range of benefits including:

An extensive wellbeing program

a free onsite gym and bike storage
annual vaccinations, skin checks, seasonal fruit and corporate cup challenge
discounted corporate private health insurance
and a proactive EAP provider
Flexible work options including part time and working from home
Access to learning and development programs to enhance your personal and professional skill set and career development
Modern offices with excellent amenities in the Adelaide CBD

Inclusion and acceptance

ReturnToWorkSA is an equal opportunity employer, valuing difference and embracing diversity and we promote flexible ways of working, applicants are encouraged to discuss any workplace adjustments that might be needed as well as the flexible working arrangements for this role.

Still not sure?

We firmly believe that diversity enhances the strength of every team, so even if you don’t tick all the boxes we still want to hear from you.

Are you ready to shape the future of data architecture and help build a modern, scalable, and insights-driven enterprise?

We’re seeking an experienced Senior Data Architect to lead the design and delivery of next-generation data solutions — from data lakes and integration frameworks to advanced analytics platforms.

As the Senior Data Architect, you’ll play a key role in defining and delivering the organisation’s enterprise data architecture. In addition, you’ll ensure data is structured, governed, and leveraged effectively to support operational and strategic decision-making.

You’ll provide architectural leadership across integration, data warehousing, analytics, and governance while fostering collaboration between business units, technical teams, and senior leadership.

Key Responsibilities of the Senior Data Architect will include:
Develop and maintain enterprise-wide data architecture strategies, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with business goals.
Lead solution design for data platforms, integrations, data lakes, and BI/reporting systems using modern architectures (batch, streaming, APIs, microservices).
Create conceptual, logical, and physical data models across structured and unstructured data using best-practice frameworks (Inmon, Kimball, Data Vault).
Define and enforce data standards for quality, metadata, security, privacy, and compliance.
Architect data solutions across Azure, Snowflake, Informatica, and hybrid/multi-cloud environments.
Collaborate with stakeholders and technical teams to translate business needs into data-driven solutions.
Guide and mentor engineering teams on CI/CD, automation, and modern data engineering practices.
Optimise data systems for performance, scalability, and reliability.
Promote and embed a strong data culture across the organisation.
Skills & Experience required to measure success:
7+ years’ experience in data architecture, solution design, and enterprise data management.
Proven success architecting large-scale BI, analytics, and integration platforms.
Strong knowledge of cloud and hybrid ecosystems (Azure, AWS, Snowflake, Informatica, Databricks).
Expertise in data modelling (Inmon, Kimball, Data Vault 2.0).
Experience with data engineering tools, CI/CD, and DevOps practices.
Deep understanding of governance, metadata, and master data management.
Experience with modern integration approaches (APIs, event-driven, streaming).
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills.
Strong analytical, problem-solving, and leadership capability

Qualifications:
Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or a related field (required).
Postgraduate qualification in Data Science or Information Systems (highly regarded).
Certifications in Microsoft, Azure, Snowflake, or Informatica (desirable).

The Metcash story began from the humble origins of our first family-owned corner store in 1927. And for almost 100 years since then, we've been backing independents and strengthening the communities we operate in.

Metcash is Australia's leading wholesale distribution company, with $19.5 billion in sales (FY25) and a proud spot in the ASX Top 125.

About Us

Future Secure AI is a rapidly scaling AI company co‑developed with one of the world’s largest financial institutions. Operating with the agility of a startup and the backing of global enterprise leaders, we are building technology that elevates human performance through advanced AI Co-workers and next‑generation LLM systems.

With deep C‑suite access across industries and a growing network of global partners, our mission is bold: to redefine how humans and AI collaborate—and deliver 10x outcomes for individuals and organisations.

We are growing fast and looking for exceptional talent to help shape the future of AI.

The Opportunity

As a Senior Data Scientist, you will be a key technical leader within our advanced analytics and AI group. You will design, build, and deploy sophisticated machine learning systems—particularly LLM‑driven solutions—that solve high‑value, real‑world problems for enterprise clients.

You’ll be part of a culture that prizes technical excellence, creativity, and continuous learning. Beyond technical depth, you will influence strategy, mentor emerging talent, and help shape our approach to responsible and scalable AI.

Your work will directly contribute to AI systems that have the potential to transform industries—and society.

What You’ll Do

Architect, build, and deploy machine learning models and LLM systems that drive meaningful impact for our products and users

Analyse complex, large‑scale structured and unstructured datasets to uncover insights and opportunities

Enhance LLM output quality across diverse use cases, including optimisation of multi‑step reasoning chains

Lead MLOps initiatives including model performance tracking, benchmark design, quality assurance, and compliance

Influence long‑term data and AI strategy, including governance, ethical AI, and scalable frameworks

Collaborate closely with engineering, product, and executive stakeholders to deliver innovative, production‑ready solutions

Mentor and uplift junior data scientists, setting standards for excellence

About You

You are a seasoned Data Scientist (5+ years) with deep hands‑on experience and a track record of delivering impactful ML and AI outcomes. You thrive in ambiguity, communicate exceptionally well, and enjoy partnering with cross‑functional teams to deliver measurable results.

You balance scientific rigour with practical execution, and you bring a curiosity that keeps you constantly exploring new research—especially in the fast‑moving LLM space.

You’ll be successful if you have:

A Master’s or Doctorate in Data Science, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science or a related discipline

Experience from within AI sector, ideally agentic AI

Strong experience with NLP techniques: LSTMs, CNNs, transformers, topic modelling, supervised NLP

Commercial experience with modern LLM ecosystems (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, RAG pipelines, multi‑agent frameworks, GPT‑based systems)

Python experience focused on machine learning and NLP. Knowledge of other software languages is highly beneficial

Ability to translate complex technical concepts into clear, actionable insights for non‑technical stakeholders

Empathy, humility, and a growth mindset—open to feedback and committed to collaboration

Comfort working in an agile, fast‑evolving environment

Ability to attend our Sydney CBD office 1–2 days per week

Why Join Us?

Shape the future of AI alongside world‑class technical and commercial leaders

Work with cutting‑edge models, frameworks, and R&D pipelines

Contribute to solutions used by global enterprises

Grow rapidly in a company with significant backing and momentum

Join a high‑trust, high‑performance culture built around craft, ownership, and impact

If you’re driven by innovation, inspired by big challenges, and ready to build AI systems that matter—we’d love to meet you.

For a quicker response, we encourage you to apply directly through our website at https://www.futuresecure.ai/. There, you can watch a video about AI workers and explore the career opportunities we have available.
